summary: CLI ships as obfuscated single-file bundle
details: The package's main entry dist/agent-flow.js is a ~1MB single-line bundle processed with javascript-obfuscator (string-array + rotation, _0xNNNN identifiers), making the published artifact effectively unauditable despite the source being available on GitHub under MIT. No install/preinstall/postinstall lifecycle scripts are declared, and tracing of decoded strings did not surface remote code fetch, eval of decoded blobs, reads of installer secrets (~/.npmrc, ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, environment scraping), or network destinations beyond Next.js documentation URLs. Standard Node imports (fs, os, child_process) are present but not wired to exfiltration or dropper paths. The concern is transparency: an obfuscated published artifact prevents downstream consumers from verifying that the shipped bytes match the public source, and reduces the value of source review for security-sensitive installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
